cmd/jobs/ledger/backfillaccounts.purpose: Single-file Cobra sub-command that backfills missing ledger accounts (business and customer) for a namespace. Intentionally bypasses the Wire DI surface to construct concrete ledger adapters directly, because the wired accountResolver is a noop when credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: Bypass DI for concrete ledger stack
patterns.description: Construct accountRepo, accountSvc, resolverRepo, and accountResolver directly from internal.App.EntClient. Never use the wired resolver from app/common — it is a noop when credits are disabled.
patterns.example: accountRepo := accountadapter.NewRepo(internal.App.EntClient); accountSvc := accountservice.New(accountRepo, locker); resolverRepo := resolversadapter.NewRepo(internal.App.EntClient); accountResolver := resolvers.NewAccountResolver(...)

patterns.name: Flags in init(), logic in RunE
patterns.description: All flag vars are package-level, registered in init() with Cmd.Flags(). RunE constructs the service, resolves inputs from flags, calls service.Run, prints the summary, then checks FailureCount for a non-zero exit.

patterns.name: RFC3339 time parsing for flag values
patterns.description: Time-valued flags are passed as strings and parsed with time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ...) in RunE, not in flag.Value; always normalize to UTC.
patterns.example: parsed, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, createdBefore); parsed = parsed.UTC(); createdBeforeTime = &parsed

patterns.name: Print summary before returning error
patterns.description: printSummary is always called before any error return so partial results are visible even on failure.
patterns.example: if err != nil { printSummary(output); return err }; printSummary(output)

patterns.name: Non-zero exit on FailureCount
patterns.description: After printing the summary, check output.Result.FailureCount and return a descriptive error so the process exits non-zero when any customer provisioning failed.
patterns.example: if output.Result.FailureCount > 0 { return fmt.Errorf("backfill completed with %d failures", output.Result.FailureCount) }

key_file_guides.role: Single-file Cobra command: package-level flag vars, init() registration, newService() that builds concrete adapters, RunE wiring, and printSummary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: internal.App is the global app struct from cmd/jobs/internal. Do not import app/common wired services — the wired accountResolver is a noop when credits.enabled=false. lockr.NewLocker must be created here and passed to accountservice.New.

anti_patterns: Using the Wire-provided ledger account resolver from app/common (it is a noop when credits.enabled=false)
Omitting lockr.NewLocker — accountservice.New requires a Locker for CreateCustomerAccounts
Parsing time flags outside RunE or omitting UTC normalization
Returning an error before printSummary (partial results become invisible)
Adding business logic inside RunE beyond input construction, service.Run invocation, and summary printing

decisions.decision: Build concrete ledger adapters directly instead of using DI outputs
decisions.rationale: AGENTS.md explicitly states: 'When writing a backfill that genuinely needs ledger writes, build the concrete adapters directly instead of relying on DI defaults (they are noops when credits.enabled=false).'

decisions.decision: Delegate all pagination and provisioning logic to cmd/jobs/ledger/service
decisions.rationale: Keeps the Cobra command thin and testable: the service package contains pure-Go logic with no Cobra/Ent imports, enabling unit tests without a real database.

code_examples.scenario: Construct the concrete ledger stack and run the backfill from RunE

cmd/jobs/ledger/service.purpose: Pure-Go backfill orchestration service (no Cobra, no direct Ent import in service.go) that pages through customers and provisions missing ledger accounts. Separated from the command layer to allow unit testing without a real database.

patterns.name: Interface-driven dependencies, no Ent in service.go
patterns.description: Service depends on unexported customerLister and accountProvisioner interfaces. Concrete implementations (EntCustomerLister, real resolver) are injected from the command layer. Never import *entdb.Client in service.go.
patterns.example: type customerLister interface { ListCustomers(ctx context.Context, input ListCustomersInput) (ListCustomersResult, error) }

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() and constructor returning error
patterns.description: All dependencies bundled in Config, validated in Config.Validate(), constructor returns (*Service, error). Add new dependencies to Config and validate them; never add optional fields with zero-value fallback.
patterns.example: func NewService(cfg Config) (*Service, error) { if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid backfill config: %w", err) }; return &Service{...}, nil }

patterns.name: RunInput.normalized() for caller-visible defaults
patterns.description: Input defaults (CustomerPageSize=0 → DefaultCustomerPageSize, CreatedBefore UTC normalization) are applied by a private normalized() method inside Run, not in the command layer or constructor.
patterns.example: func (i RunInput) normalized() RunInput { out := i; if out.CustomerPageSize == 0 { out.CustomerPageSize = DefaultCustomerPageSize }; return out }

patterns.name: paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER guard on all cursor loops
patterns.description: All cursor-paginated loops must check against paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER and call recordFailure + return error when exhausted, preventing infinite loops on bad cursors.
patterns.example: for iter := 0; iter < paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER; iter++ { ...; if !completed { return result, s.recordFailure(&result, "paginate_customers", "", fmt.Errorf("max safe iter reached")) } }

patterns.name: Idempotent Get-then-Create provisioning with ValidationIssue code detection
patterns.description: Always call GetCustomerAccounts / GetBusinessAccounts first. Only provision when the error carries ledger.ErrCodeCustomerAccountMissing / ErrCodeBusinessAccountMissing via hasValidationIssueCode; other errors go through recordFailure. Makes the backfill safely re-runnable.
patterns.example: if !hasValidationIssueCode(err, ledger.ErrCodeCustomerAccountMissing) { return s.recordFailure(result, "get_customer_accounts", customerID.ID, err) }

patterns.name: DryRun increments WouldProvision counters only
patterns.description: When input.DryRun is true, increment BusinessWouldProvision / CustomersWouldProvision and return without calling EnsureBusinessAccounts / CreateCustomerAccounts.

patterns.name: recordFailure owns FailureCount increment and slog.Warn; ContinueOnError checked in caller
patterns.description: recordFailure always increments result.FailureCount and logs via slog.Warn. Callers check input.ContinueOnError to decide whether to return the error or continue.
patterns.example: failure := s.recordFailure(&result, "list_customers", "", err); if input.ContinueOnError { return result, nil }; return result, failure

key_file_guides.role: Core orchestration: Config/Service types, customerLister/accountProvisioner interfaces, Run/runNamespace/ensureBusinessAccounts/ensureCustomerAccounts/recordFailure/hasValidationIssueCode.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No direct Ent imports — keep this file free of *entdb.Client. Do not add new ledger error codes without updating hasValidationIssueCode. ContinueOnError must be checked by callers of recordFailure, not inside recordFailure itself.

key_file_guides.role: EntCustomerLister implements customerLister using Ent cursor queries (query.Cursor) with namespace + DeletedAt + CreatedAt filters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses query.Cursor(ctx, input.Cursor) — Ent cursor pagination, not offset. IncludeDeleted=false filters via customerdb.Or(DeletedAtIsNil(), DeletedAtGTE(now)) for correct soft-delete semantics. CreatedBefore filter uses CreatedAtLT with UTC normalization.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests using fakeCustomerLister and fakeAccountProvisioner covering dry-run, cursor pagination, created-before cutoff, and continue-on-error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses t.Context() not context.Background(). fakeCustomerLister implements cursor pagination manually — keep consistent with real cursor semantics (time+ID ordering) when adding tests. fakeAccountProvisioner uses ledger.ErrCustomerAccountMissing.WithAttrs(...) to emit real ValidationIssue codes.

anti_patterns: Importing *entdb.Client directly in service.go — breaks unit testability
Checking ContinueOnError inside recordFailure — callers own that decision
Skipping the MAX_SAFE_ITER guard in any new paginated loop
Using offset pagination instead of paginationv2.Cursor for customer iteration
Using context.Background() in tests instead of t.Context()

decisions.decision: Separate service package from Cobra command
decisions.rationale: Enables pure-Go unit tests (no Ent, no Cobra, no real DB) covering dry-run, cursor paging, cutoff filtering, and error continuation without integration overhead.

decisions.decision: Detect missing-account condition via ValidationIssue error codes, not nil checks
decisions.rationale: ledger.ErrCodeCustomerAccountMissing / ErrCodeBusinessAccountMissing are structured error codes on ValidationIssue; treating any non-nil error as 'missing' would mask real failures from DB or network errors.

code_examples.scenario: Cursor-paginated customer provisioning loop with DryRun and ContinueOnError
